titleStmt.title: Letter from Charles E. Fair to the Under-Secretary, 26 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Charles E. Fair

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Charles E. Fair to Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938), Under-Secretary for Ireland, following up on earlier correspondence and enclosing a testimonial letter from Captain Tottenham of Tudenham Park, Mullingar, County Westmeath, in support of his application for the position of Clerk of Crown and Peace for County Westmeath.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5615/10839

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
ATHLONE, 26th day of June, 1916. Sir,— Enclosed recommendation in the matter
of my application for appointment of
Clerk of the Crown and Peace for County
Westmeath was received by me this morning
From Capt Tottenham D.L, Tudenham,
Mullingar, Your obedservant C E Fair The Under Secretary, —
Dublin Castle. I Ackged with
10765
II Add to file
EAof
27.6.16
24 thJune 1916 I have known Solr
Charles E Fair for
some time I dont
remember how many
years he has acted
as my Agent for two
or three years to my
entire satisfaction,
I understand that he
is applying
for the Clerkship
of the Crown and Peace for
Westmeath and I consider
that, if appointed he
will carry out the duties.
